Once you have regained control, there be a ton of side activities you need to do first. Go to Trista, near the **entrance of the Upper Class Dorm**, speak to **Lotte** and she will teach you the **Tomato Pot** recipe. Then go back to Thors and enter the **school library**. Speak to the Librarian **Karol**, she will give you the Finale of Gambler Jack II. If you have collected all chapters of Gambler Jack II, head to **Kirsche's Cafe & Inn** and speak to **Vice Principal Heinrich**, he will give you a **Zemurian Ore**. This will allow you to forge the strongest weapon in the game. Then speak to him again to update his profile. There are also **two fishing spots in Trista**, one is the **pond at the north end** of the school, the other is the river at the northeast of town. Then head to the **house located northeast of Trista** and examine the bookshelf. You can collect the **Fiery Colour** for your bike, it is to the right of the staircase on the bottom floor. Speak to all the **NPCs** listed below to update their character profiles (There is a lot!) After you have spoken to all of them, you should speak to Alfin at the principal office to get the **Medal of Love** if you haven't received it yet. Afterwards, take the **west exit of Trista** to see an event. Then speak to **Towa at the school rooftop**. Then read the [Chosen Partner Event](https://www.neoseeker.com/the-legend-of-heroes-trails-of-cold-steel-ii/walkthrough/Finale#Chosen_Partner_Event "walkthrough/Finale") section just below this guide.

### December 31st

Rean will learn his final S-Craft. Then read all the side quests from the computer terminal. Then head to the 1st floor and speak to George, you can use that Zemurian Ore to craft the strongest weapon in the game.

| Nightmare Recommendations |
| --- |
| Give the Zemurian Ore weapons to Rean and Laura. These two are crucial for the Cancelling strategy to go faster (especially Laura's). |

Now there will be a ton of side activities you need to do before you start the main story. Update everyone's character profile listed below.

At this point of the game, you should have collected all the recipes. Try to cook every type of superb, unique and peculiar dishes. Then **give all superb dishes to Monica** at the 2nd floor Cafeteria. When you give her all 22 superb dishes, she will give you the **Impenetrable Shield** quartz. Then speak **Margarita** at the northern end of the corridor and **give her all peculiar dishes**, she will give you the **Chaos** quartz. If you have discovered all 22 recipes, speak to **Ferris** in the Game Room, she will give you the [Genesis](https://www.neoseeker.com/the-legend-of-heroes-trails-of-cold-steel-ii/walkthrough/Arts_and_Quartz#Genesis "walkthrough/Arts and Quartz") master quartz. Then go to the 3rd floor and speak to **Bridget** and **give her every type of Unique dishes**, she will give you the **Revolution** quartz.

If this is your **second playthrough** and **have collected all the Black Records books**, speak to **Instructor Thomas** at the Book Room (2F) and give him all the Black Records, you will get a trophy later for this. On the off chance you failed to get one of the Black Records there is a small chance Micht in Trista or Becky on the third floor of the Courageous could have one in the Curios section, leaving Micht's store and entering again refreshes his stock and gives you another chance of the book appearing so keep doing this until it appears. Then head to Ymir and speak to Rean's father at the Baron's Mansion, this will also unlock an optional dungeon called the **Glacia Shrine** located in the very west end of **Ymir Valley 1**. Defeating the [boss](https://www.neoseeker.com/the-legend-of-heroes-trails-of-cold-steel-ii/walkthrough/Optional_Bosses#Isra-Zamiel "walkthrough/Optional Bosses") in this optional dungeon will give you the **Ice Crown** accessory that gives a permanent 10 CP Regeneration per turn and a +50% Critical Up status effect and also **The Frost Hunter** trophy.

After this, the first thing you need to do is finish all the training module inside Courageous. Clearing all 5 stages of each training module will give you a master quartz.

Speak to Towa, she should give you the [Emblem](https://www.neoseeker.com/the-legend-of-heroes-trails-of-cold-steel-ii/walkthrough/Arts_and_Quartz#Emblem "walkthrough/Arts and Quartz") Master Quartz for recruiting all students for Courageous. Then head to **Ymir**, if you have caught 23 different types of fishes, speak to **Annabelle in Ymir Valley** for an event. This will let you catch the last fish, **Arch Tyrant**. Catching the final fish will let you purchase the **Aeolus Gem** from her. There is also a **new course added to the snow boarding mini game**. Clearing it will give you the **Skanda Gem**. Return to Courageous and speak to Kenneth at the Cafeteria, he will give you the [Orochi](https://www.neoseeker.com/the-legend-of-heroes-trails-of-cold-steel-ii/walkthrough/Arts_and_Quartz#Orochi "walkthrough/Arts and Quartz") Master quartz if you caught the Arch Tyrant from earlier. You can also purchase the **Sennin Gem** from him.

After you have finished that, work on the side quests. Completing each quest will give you a **Zemurian Ore Shard**, which means you can get another ultimate weapon when finishing all side quests.

| One of the easiest boss, you can use Canceler build to prevent the boss from having even a single turn. Another method would be using attacks that cause faint on the boss, they work quite effectively against the boss. |

However, it does have the strongest attack, it has a Thunder wave which can deal a massive damage to your group and also have a chance of muting one of your party members. This boss is also very weak against mute as well. You can disable most of it's attack by silencing it. The Canceler build work great on this boss. Due the boss' low HP, a full party S-Craft can instantly wipe out the boss. |

Once you defeated the boss, you will get a Zemurian Ore Shard.

But with all the arsenal you have right now, you can easily wipe it out with the Canceler Strategy, preventing the boss from having even a single turn. If you don't have that setup, an effectively method would be causing the Confuse ailment on the boss. |

Return to Courageous and use the Zemurian Ore Shards to create another ultimate weapon. Now go Trista, there will be some side activities you can still do. Speak to **Beatrix** at the Main Building Infirmary, this will update her character profile. Then speak to the head priest at Trista's Chapel. Accept the request to start a hidden quest.

####  [![QuesticonCS2.gif](https://cdn.staticneo.com/ew/c/c1/QuesticonCS2.gif)](https://www.neoseeker.com/the-legend-of-heroes-trails-of-cold-steel-ii/File:QuesticonCS2.gif) Hidden Quest: Absentee Father

* * *

Accept the request from Father Paulo. Then, speak to the head priest in each major town or city. If you bring **Sara and Gaius** with you, there will be some slight changes in the final scene. You will need to speak to the priests in this order to complete the quest:

1.  Celdic Chapel - Zirbel
2.  Bareahard Cathedral - Amon
3.  Legram Chapel - Hamilcar
4.  Roer Cathedral - Homs
5.  Ymir Chapel - Bernard

| Client | Father Paulo |
| --- | --- |
| Reward and AP | Acala Gem, 4500 Mira, 4 AP |

Once your finished the quest, use the Courageous computer and report your quest. You should get the gold trophy for reaching **315 AP** along with the **Lion Heart** accessory.

You can also explore the other half of East Trista Highway. There will be some new monsters that can be filled in the battle notes, and also some unopened treasure chest.

At this point, you should have collected **284 battle notes**. Go and speak to Stefan in the computer room (2F). He will give you the [Calamity](https://www.neoseeker.com/the-legend-of-heroes-trails-of-cold-steel-ii/walkthrough/Arts_and_Quartz#Calamity "walkthrough/Arts and Quartz") master quartz. If you fought all the monsters until now and you have 283 battle notes then most probably you are missing Metal Beetle which is available after December 18 in Twin Dragon Bridge - Garrelia Side.

There are also some optional story scenes that you can see, such as visiting **Scarlet**, Duke Albarea in the Albarea Mansion (Bareahard) with Jusis, the resting area in Proving Ground, and speaking to **Professor Schmitt** in Kirsche (Trista). Because this will be the final time you will be able to use the Courageous, make sure you have finished everything you need to do before starting the operation with Towa.

| Final Mission Preparations (Fresh Nightmare Run) |

### Final Mission

* * *

Select your party members, Machias will be mandatory for this part.

**Karel Imperial Villa** Run up the hill, you will immediately fight some Alliance Forces soldiers. Defeat them. then when you reach the entrance of the villa, you will fight another group. Then go inside the villa, defeat the soldiers.

[![Trails of cold steel ii karel imperial villa 1.png](https://cdn.staticneo.com/ew/7/74/Trails_of_cold_steel_ii_karel_imperial_villa_1.png)](https://www.neoseeker.com/the-legend-of-heroes-trails-of-cold-steel-ii/File:Trails_of_cold_steel_ii_karel_imperial_villa_1.png) [![Trails of cold steel ii karel imperial villa 2.png](https://cdn.staticneo.com/ew/3/31/Trails_of_cold_steel_ii_karel_imperial_villa_2.png)](https://www.neoseeker.com/the-legend-of-heroes-trails-of-cold-steel-ii/File:Trails_of_cold_steel_ii_karel_imperial_villa_2.png)

Heal up using the Orbment Station in front of you. Then go upstairs and pick up the two treasure chests. The one on the north contains **Claiomh Solarion** and the one on the south is a **Seraphic Ring**. Go back downstairs, and take the north exit to outside. You will find three treasures, some **U-Materials**, **Tear All Balm**, and a **Goldia Key** in a monster chest. Exit out of this area, and then take the south entrance to Villa 3, you will fight another group of soldiers. Use the Golden Key to unlock the door, travel to the north end, you will fight a large group of soldiers. The Tough Enemies in here (the Sleipnirs) and the boss have high Def and HP, but neither uses arts so you might want to use Emma or Elliot with the Aries Master Quartz and Shadow Blade (both get the Might Set) since their ADF is not that high. Laura, with [Warrior Set](https://www.neoseeker.com/the-legend-of-heroes-trails-of-cold-steel-ii/walkthrough/Advance_Strategy_-_Stacking_\(In-Depth\)#Warrior "walkthrough/Advance Strategy - Stacking (In-Depth)") and a Zemurian Ore Weapon works wonders on them. When you reach to the exit, defeat the large group of enemies. Then heal up with the orbment station next to the door. Go inside to fight the boss.

| McBurn is perhaps the strongest and the hardest boss of Cold Steel II. No matter what strategy you are using, this fight requires some luck, as McBurn will use his S-Craft near the beginning of the fight. He also can use the Sol Eruption Lost Art, which must be canceled. Both his S-Craft and the Lost Arts are unblockable, but the S-Craft's damage can be reduced with Def Up. You can inflict Spd/Str/Def down on him if you have the respective Stacks or use Chrono Burst. That is your only room for work on this fight since his crafts will cancel your buffs and you still have a 50-50 chance of getting hit even with Insight as only Fie will be able to dodge them completely. While Purgatorial Flame can be reflected, you are better off canceling since it does laughable damage to McBurn, and Arc Slash will hurt him more. As for his special Burn status, only two characters will suffer from it if you have both Lionheart and Grail Locket. You must cure it on the other 2 with items ASAP since it chops a good amount of HP per turn and it won't fade until the character dies or the status is cured. As with the previous battle, Artemis' Tear will give you that extra HP you need in case you fail to buff against the S-Craft or cancel the Lost Art. Speed is the key to this battle so McBurn gets as few turns as possible. When McBurn's HP Mark drops to 100 000 HP, using Radiant Lion and Termination Slash - Dawn on Ogre Mode at max CP can deal enough damage to finish him off. |

| This boss can be hard since he has 5 different attacks, and only 2 stances: Normal and Charging. He can unbalance AND counter you, do them both in succession, and when he has unbalanced you 4 times, he WILL use one of his 2 Follow Ups (just like you). The second one will come after 5 consecutive unbalances, and it hits harder. Luckily, he only starts using that one on the second half of the fight. Not so luckily is the fact that he will mix both, but you can tell what is coming your way IF he does nothing to you on the 4th unbalance. He is fast and will combo you often.You COULD learn what he is going to do by listening to what he says, but two of his attacks (one of them a really damaging one) share the same quote. so the best way to go at this fight is to build CP for Morning Moon and counter him. There is, however, a way to know what he is going to do. These are his attacks:Normal Attack (weak: Arm)Charge 1 (Weak: Body)Charge 2 (Weak: Head)*Charge 3 (Weak: Arm)Charge 4 (Weak: Head)**Note: These two share the same quote.Ordine's pattern will start with 1, then 2, then 3 and back to 1. After, you chop 50-60% of Ordine's HP, then it will use the pattern of 1, then 2, then 3, then 4, then 5 and back to 1. It is easier to figure which attack is coming after if you memorize his pattern. If you miss the beat (and you will) just look at his unbalance weakness. When you see Head as the most likely, use Morning Moon (or guard if you want to save CP). When Charge 3 comes around (Weak: Arm) you can get 2 good shots out of Morning Moon AND dodge his deadliest attacks, which incidentally are the ones where he will aim to unbalance and Rush/Burst you. Your best supports for this one are Elliot (healing), Alisa (CP Regen) and Sara (Str down on the boss). |

Watch the event scene, afterwards the game will give you the option to save for the final boss. Assign your party members, there is several phase to the final boss fight, whoever you did not pick with start on the first round of the fight and whoever you assigned for your party will come at the second phase. Both phase are difficult boss fights, the first phase is overall harder, while the second phase hits harder but is also easier to deal with.

| Strategies |
| Baiting Strategy: Your safest bet if you have Fie. She must immediately go and attack the boss while your party regroups and buff themselves. Have Sara or Gaius attack with Thunder Roar and Gale Storm. Refrain from using Flash EX and Savage Fang II since those attacks will mess up your placement, making it harder to buff with arts if you run out of Food Items. Elliot and Machias are in charge of healing, buffing, and in case of Machias using Burst Drive to mess up the boss' turn order. Have one of them use Impassion on Fie so she can use her crafts since she is the fastest character and will go through her CP at an astounding pace.S-Craft: Send Fie up front and attack the boss with Bullet Cyclone II EVERY TURN while you buff Gaius and use Twin Cyclones (S-Craft). Then have him use Wild Rage ONCE, and feed him a Seasonal Hotpot. The other two characters in the back row will alternate between supporting Fie and Gaius throughout the fight.Cancelling: Regroup the WHOLE PARTY (yes, you read right) right next to the boss. Cast Resonance Beat and start spamming Chrono Drive and Chrono Break with Elliot and keep the boss fully Spd debuffed. This way Bullet Cyclone II, Flash EX and Savage Fang II will both hurt him and have a chance of Delaying him, giving you more turns to top your HP. If either Fie or Sara successfully Delay the boss, then Gaius can use an S-Craft. Mind you, this last bit can be a risk, since Gaius won't be able to join in the Delaying attempts, do so with care and only if you can ensure he can recover his CP quickly. |
| The boss can cast a Craft and Art Reflect barrier in himself, in which case have Fie and Elliot use their weakest craft and art to dispel it. Either with Holy Song or Seraphic Ring (Aries MQ ensure Elliot recovers plenty of EP every time he attacks the boss with arts) make sure you top your HP before the boss takes his turns, since he has ALL hitting attacks/arts that can cripple or downright kill your weakest members if they are not properly buffed. For that reason, Elliot's Resonance Beat is also immensely helpful. If you want to make sure he gets enough turns to do all he has to do, have Machias, which will be the slowest, use Burst Drive. Many times, that will represent the difference between surviving and Game Over. This is the hardest of all the Rounds. So hang in there. When the boss' HP reach to 60 000 HP. You can spam your S-Craft to finish the boss off. |
